flags.concern: Effect preview wrapping: the plan's proposed canonical helper signature is too narrow to replace the preview path as written. `makePreviewComposition()` wraps a compiled `EffectComponent` directly rather than looking up a type in a built-in map, so Step 3 cannot call a helper shaped as `(content, effectType, builtInMap, config)` without redesigning that API.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:143-169` takes `EffectComponent: FC<EffectComponentProps> | null` and renders it directly. The existing shared logic in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:99-181` is type/map lookup based, so the Step 1 helper contract described in the plan does not actually cover the preview code path it claims to unify in Step 3.

flags.concern: Effect contract: forcing `effectFrames` to `undefined` for continuous effects is a breaking contract change for custom effects. The current preview and effect-layer paths both pass the full duration for continuous effects, and custom effect templates already consume `effectFrames`, so switching those callers to `undefined` would silently change runtime behavior for existing continuous custom effects.
flags.evidence: Continuous preview currently sets `effectFrames = durationInFrames` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:152-167`, and `EffectLayerSequence` passes `effectFrames={durationInFrames}` in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:41-48`. The legacy custom template in `src/tools/video-editor/components/CustomEffectEditor.tsx:18` defaults to `effectFrames = 20`, so removing the full-duration value would make continuous custom effects fall back to that internal default instead of the layer/preview length.

flags.concern: Effect registry concurrency: the proposed `stale` boolean in `useEffectRegistry` does not actually close the async overwrite race. Once an old `registerAsync()` call has started compiling, its awaited result still writes into the shared registry after completion, so an older effect payload can overwrite a newer one even if the outer effect has already been cleaned up.
flags.evidence: `useEffectRegistry` currently awaits `registry.registerAsync(...)` inside sequential loops at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:40-48`. `DynamicEffectRegistry.registerAsync()` writes `this.dynamic[normalized] = { component, code, schema }` only after `await compileEffectAsync(code)` in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:53-59`. A cleanup-scoped `stale` check before each await cannot stop an already-started older compile from committing after a newer registration finishes.

flags.concern: Effect layers: the plan touches `EffectLayerSequence` but misses that effect-layer animations are using the global Remotion frame instead of a frame relative to the layer start. Any continuous effect layer that starts after frame 0 will begin mid-animation or fully progressed, so the refactor would preserve a visible timing bug in one of the main files under review.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:22-29` gates on `frame < startFrame || frame >= endFrame` but then renders the effect without offsetting frame context in `:41-48`. The built-in continuous effects read `useCurrentFrame()` directly and derive progress from that frame in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/continuous.tsx:5-10` and `:31-43`, so a layer starting later on the timeline does not animate from its own frame 0.
